parallel--897c37d6-01c6-480d-87fa-4a6e02.name: Implement correlation logic to flag sequential probing behavior:

description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1046 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0376#AN1057 . The description of the approach follows: Detects processes performing network enumeration (e.g., port scans, service probing) by correlating process creation, socket connections, and sequential destination IP probing within a time window.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1046: Network Service Discovery
MITRE ATT&CK Detection Strategy DET0376: Network Service Discovery

items.content: Deploy and configure a SIEM or log aggregation tool to collect and correlate Windows event logs (e.g., Security, Sysmon) and network data.
Enable and configure Sysmon logging with a focus on Event ID 1 (Process creation) and Event ID 3 (Network connection).
Enable Windows Security auditing for process creation (Audit Process Creation) and network connections if not using Sysmon.
Configure network monitoring tools (e.g., Zeek, Suricata) to log outbound connections and detect scanning patterns.
Create a detection rule to identify processes that create multiple network connections to different destination IPs or ports within a short time window (e.g., 5-10 minutes).
Group network connection events by source process and timestamp.
Analyze for high counts of unique destination IPs or ports per process over a defined period.
Set thresholds to reduce false positives (e.g., >50 unique IPs/ports in 5 minutes).
Implement correlation logic to flag sequential probing behavior:
Tune the detection to exclude legitimate administrative or network scanning tools used by the organization.
Set up alerts for detected enumeration activity, including details like process name, command line, and destination IPs/ports.
Integrate alerts with incident response workflows for rapid investigation and containment.
